address: 31 St Hellier Street, Heidelberg Heights VIC 3081
status: Good contender, location-led rather than layout-led

listing.agent: Paul Carbone, Miles Real Estate Ivanhoe & Rosanna

layout.estimated_under_roof_area_m2: Property.com.au reports 166m2 floor area; visual floorplan estimate is 140-155m2 excluding open deck, depending on what is counted

rooms.name: Lounge
rooms.bathroom_access: Near entry and main bedroom zone

rooms.name: Kitchen
rooms.storage: Three-sided kitchen, extra-large pantry, soft-close drawers, extensive bench space

orientation.north_arrow_interpretation: Floorplan and listing text indicate the lounge is north-facing. With north at the top of the floorplan, the rear garden/deck is broadly south-facing, while side windows serve east/west light depending on exact boundary orientation.
best_light_rooms: Dining / living through large side and rear openings

strengths: Excellent Heidelberg Heights location relative to hospitals, Burgundy Street, Heidelberg Station, La Trobe University and inner-north anchors.
Single-level layout is practical and easy to live in.
North-facing lounge is a strong light and winter-comfort feature.
Kitchen storage is excellent for a compact single-level home.
Bedroom 2 and Bedroom 3 share a central bathroom with a separate toilet.
Double garage is better than Buick's single garage.
watchpoints: No dedicated study; a work area would need to use Bedroom 3, lounge, dining area, or garage conversion/storage zone.
Bedroom 3 is only 7.5m2 and is better as a nursery/small study than a spacious bedroom.
Main bedroom is modest at 10.9m2; ensuite is clean and contemporary but compact.
Garden is beautiful but likely higher maintenance than Buick's covered alfresco setup.
Price guide is materially higher than Buick while giving less internal flexible space.
next_action: Only pursue if location premium matters more than dedicated study and room size; verify auction result/current availability because auction was scheduled for 2 May 2026.
